resources.string: From the empty eyes, bright mushrooms sprout like a grateful spring
BRIGHT MUSHROOMS RISE FROM EMPTY EYES

How generous the fallen field has become. Yellow-green spores roll through the mist, fat white grubs turn the soil, and the great slime mold beats softly under the blossoms as if it has borrowed a heart. Beneath the sweet flowers lie the old bodies, no longer wasted, no longer alone. Every rib is a cradle, every wound a seedbed, and every quiet soldier has become a banquet for a million smaller lives.
FALLEN FIELD REPORT. SPORES ROLL THROUGH MIST. GRUBS TURN THE SOIL. EVERY RIB IS A CRADLE. EVERY WOUND IS A SEEDBED. THE FEAST OPENS FOR SMALLER LIVES.
